title: Combustion system component formed by metallic additive layer manufacture and method of manufacturing thereof
abstract: A combustion system component formed by metallic additive layer manufacture wherein the component has a flow-developing surface (SC) having an arithmetic average surface roughness (R a ) of between 3 and 10Âµm.
claims:
Claims Claims 1. A combustion system component formed by metallic additive layer manufacture wherein the component has a flow-developing surface having an arithmetic average surface roughness (R a ) of between 3 and lOpm. 2. The combustion system component of claim 1, wherein the flow developing surface has an arithmetic average surface roughness (R a ) of between 4.5 and 5.5pm. 3. The combustion system component of claim 1 or 2, wherein the component is a nickel-based superalloy. 4. A combustion system component as claimed in any preceding claim, wherein the flow-developing surface extends along at least one plane which is generally parallel to a build direction of the additive layer manufacture process. 5. The combustion system component of any preceding claim wherein the component is a gas turbine swirl burner and the flow-developing surface includes a surface of at least one of a swirl vane and a nozzle. 6. The combustion system component as claimed in claim 5, further comprising a swirler base. 7. The combustion system component as claimed in any of claims 1 to 6, wherein at least the flow-developing surface of the component is a post- processed surface such that the arithmetic average surface roughness (R a ) is 

 reduced relative to the average surface roughness (R a ) provided in the additive layer manufacturing process. 8. A method of manufacturing a combustion system component, the component having at least one flow developing surface, wherein the method comprising forming the at least one flow developing surface using a metallic additive layer manufacturing process in which the at least one flow developing surface extends along a plane which is parallel to the additive layer build direction. 9. A method of forming combustion system components, the method comprising forming the component using a metallic additive layer manufacturing process; and post-processing the component using abrasive blasting to reduce surface peak values and provide at least one flow-developing surface having a reduced average surface roughness (R a ). 10. The method of claim 9, wherein the post-processing comprises aluminia grit blasting followed by stainless steel bead blasting. 11. The method of claim 9 or 10, wherein the post processing reduces the average surface roughness (R a ) by between 30 to 50% of the surface roughness provided by the additive layer process. 12. The method of any of claims 9 to 11, wherein the at least one flow developing surface is non-parallel to the additive layer build direction during the additive layer manufacturing process. 13. The method of any of claims 8 to 12, wherein the at least one flow- developing surface is formed with an average surface roughness (R a ) of between 3 and IOmiti. 14. The method of any of claims 8 to 13, wherein the component is a gas turbine swirl burner and the flow-developing surface includes a surface of at least one of a swirl vane and a nozzle. 15. The method of any of claims 8 to 14, wherein the additive layer manufacturing process comprises powder bed selective laser melting.
